video_title: 3 Ways to fix Land Rover air suspension
transcript: so if your Discovery 3 Discovery 4 or Range Rover Sport looks like this then you need to sort out your air suspension ideally if your pump is gone you want to be replacing it with an amk unit now there's two different kinds of units that appear in these vehicles there's amk and there's Hitachi the Hitachi was the original older version and that requires a bracket that looks like this so we sell the replacement pumps for if you've got this bracket in your vehicle or if you're lucky enough to have amk pump Which is less likely to have broken but it could still have done so you'll have a bracket like this in your vehicle the L-shaped bracket so that's a really easy and quick way to identify what kind of pump you've got in there so if you've got a catastrophic failure it's well worth considering replacing the whole unit if you've got the oldach unit replace the whole unit with the amk kit from Brit par because you get the new bracket you get the pump and you get all the fixings if you don't want to do that and you're on a tighter budget than that then you can definitely replace your h pump with the dun loock version which is a lot better than the original so that would be my recommendation the cheapest budget version if you've got a Hitachi pump in your air suspension system is to replace it with a Dunlop one if you don't have that if you got the amk one Dunlop also do a replacement for the amk1 which is a bit cheaper than a genuine amk1 are you still with me right now the last option you have so if you decided to replace the whole system and you got the old hitach and you want to go up to the amk system you'd be looking at just under £500 plus tax which is fine but you could opt for a conversion to coils for round about the same price Dunlop do a really good conversion which is about £550 plus tax so you've got three great options there for you to keep your Discovery 3 your Discovery 4 your Range Rover Sport on the road hopefully that made some sense
